evals.name: happy-path-rtsp-capture-and-calibrate
evals.prompt: Calibrate from 3 RTSP streams: rtsp://HOST:31554/cam_00, rtsp://HOST:31555/cam_01, rtsp://HOST:31556/cam_02. Record 180 seconds. Alignment JSON and layout PNG are at /data/alignment/. Microservice at http://HOST:8000. VIOS is already running.
evals.expected_output: Skill verifies VIOS is reachable via /vst/api/v1/sensor/list, creates a project, calls POST /v1/rtsp/capture/<project_id> with the 3 streams and duration_seconds=180, polls the session through STARTING/RECORDING/COMPLETED, calls POST /v1/rtsp/capture/<project_id>/<session_id>/ingest, uploads alignment and layout, verifies READY, calibrates, polls, and reports results.

assertions.text: Skill calls the capture start endpoint
Skill ingests clips into the project after capture completes
Skill polls calibration until the COMPLETED state
Skill does not leak bearer tokens or VIOS credentials in output

evals.name: manual-alignment-after-rtsp-ingest
evals.prompt: Calibrate from RTSP: rtsp://HOST:31554/cam_00 and rtsp://HOST:31555/cam_01, 120 seconds. I don't have alignment or layout files. MS at http://HOST:8000, VIOS up.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs the capture + ingest sequence, then directs the user to open the UI and complete Step 4 alignment for the newly created project. It waits for confirmation and verifies projects/project_<id>/manual_adjustment/ contains alignment_data.json and layout.png before calling verify_project and calibrate.

assertions.text: Skill tells the user to complete alignment in the UI
Skill verifies manual alignment output on disk before calibrating
Skill refuses to calibrate without confirmed alignment
Skill does not echo vios_token or bearer credentials

evals.name: negative-vios-unreachable
evals.prompt: Calibrate from rtsp://HOST:31554/cam_00. MS is up but VIOS is not.
evals.expected_output: Skill probes VIOS at /vst/api/v1/sensor/list, detects it is unreachable, searches for a vios skill in skills/, and if no such skill is found asks the user to deploy VIOS and provide the base URL. It refuses to call /v1/rtsp/capture until VIOS confirms healthy.

assertions.text: Skill reports that VIOS is unreachable
Skill searches for a VIOS skill or asks the user
Skill does not attempt capture while VIOS is down
Skill does not embed a real VIOS bearer token in its output

evals.name: config-anchor-detector-override-alignment-autoscan
evals.prompt: Calibrate from rtsp://HOST:31554/cam_00 and rtsp://HOST:31555/cam_01 for 120s. MS at http://HOST:8000, VIOS up. I have a settings file at /data/calib/settings.json that includes detector: transformer. alignment_data.json and layout.png live in the same /data/calib/ directory.
evals.expected_output: Skill uses /data/calib/settings.json as the anchor, auto-scans that directory for alignment_data.json and layout.png, POSTs the settings file to /v1/config/<project_id>, parses it for the detector field and overrides DETECTOR_TYPE to transformer for the calibrate call, uploads alignment and layout, then continues with verify + calibrate + poll.

assertions.text: Skill uses the config file's directory as the anchor for alignment scan
Skill calls the config endpoint
Skill applies the detector override from the config file
Skill does not leak bearer tokens from config-upload or VIOS responses

evals.name: negative-vios-base-url-missing-in-ms-env
evals.prompt: Calibrate from rtsp://HOST:31554/cam_00. The capture endpoint returned 503 VIOS not configured.
evals.expected_output: Skill recognises VIOS_BASE_URL is missing from the MS container environment, instructs the user either to deploy alongside a bp_wh_* blueprint (which auto-wires it from VST_INTERNAL_URL) or set it explicitly in deploy/docker/industry-profiles/warehouse-operations/.env and re-run docker compose --env-file ... up -d from deploy/docker. Does not proceed with capture until the endpoint returns non-503.

assertions.text: Skill identifies the missing environment variable
Skill instructs the user to set the env var via the warehouse-operations .env or a bp_wh_* blueprint
Skill does not proceed with capture while the endpoint returns 503
Skill does not hardcode a credential as a fix for the 503
